name: OpenHands
what_it_does: Open source AI software engineering agent platform that autonomously writes code, runs tests, browses the web, and executes shell commands to complete software tasks.
use_cases: Autonomous bug fixing and feature implementation
Code review and refactoring via agent
not_for: Simple single-turn code completions (use Copilot/Cursor instead)
Teams needing strict audit trails on every code change
best_when: You have well-defined software tasks that require multi-step execution (write code, run tests, fix failures) and can tolerate non-deterministic agent behavior
avoid_when: You need reproducible deterministic outputs or are working in highly regulated environments where every action must be pre-approved

auth.notes: Self-hosted requires no auth by default; cloud deployment uses API key. LLM provider keys (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) required separately.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Fully free when self-hosted; cloud early access pricing TBD
pricing.paid_tiers: Cloud deployment pricing not yet publicly announced
pricing.notes: Self-hosted is free and open source (MIT). LLM provider costs apply separately based on token usage.

performance.rate_limits: Constrained by underlying LLM provider rate limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Agent surfaces LLM and sandbox errors; task-level errors reported via event stream
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Each task execution is unique; no built-in idempotency keys
known_agent_gotchas: Tasks are long-running; callers must poll event stream for completion
LLM provider rate limits can silently stall tasks
Sandbox filesystem is ephemeral unless explicitly persisted
Docker runtime required for sandboxed code execution
No official SLA for cloud deployment (early access)

security_score_components.security_notes: Code execution runs in Docker sandbox; LLM keys passed via env vars; self-hosted deployments require operator-managed network security
